preamble: Monophthongization of the diphthong [uo] to the close front unrounded vowel [i] through vowel merger and raising. This shift occurs in various Sino-Tibetan and Romance languages, representing a pathway toward high vowel targets in diphthong reduction.

examples.note: In Wu Chinese dialects, the diphthong [uo] monophthongizes to close front vowel [i] in certain phonological contexts.
In some Romance dialects, [uo] reduces to high vowel [i] in rapid speech, particularly before certain consonants.
